nodes.label: IMPORTANT: keep the reminder string free of backticks and $(...) constructs.
nodes.norm_label: important: keep the reminder string free of backticks and $(...) constructs.

nodes.label: Multi-Tenancy Isolation
nodes.rationale: Every table has a tenant_id (organization_id) foreign key. All queries filter by tenant automatically for database level isolation.

nodes.label: Per-Node Execution Model
nodes.rationale: Each workflow step is a separate BullMQ job. The worker orchestrates transitions rather than enqueuing the entire workflow, supporting non-blocking delays, retry granularity, and crash resilience.

nodes.label: Separate Job Queues
nodes.rationale: Separation into triggerQueue and executionQueue ensures scheduled triggers are processed reliably and don't get delayed by slow execution jobs.

nodes.label: React Flow Frontend Editor
nodes.rationale: React Flow (xyflow) is selected over alternatives because it is native React and makes conditional branching native and fast to implement.

nodes.label: DAG Validation
nodes.rationale: Uses graphology + graphology-dag for design-time cycle detection and topological order analysis.

nodes.label: Non-Blocking Delay
nodes.rationale: Uses BullMQ's delay option instead of blocking worker sleep(), scaling to thousands of delayed jobs.

nodes.label: State Persistence in Database
nodes.rationale: Workflow execution state is stored in workflow_runs and step_logs tables, decoupling job queue from execution history.

nodes.label: Real-Time SSE Updates
nodes.rationale: Worker publishes step updates to Redis, which the API server streams to the frontend using Server-Sent Events.
